subject: Gaming
title: Stardew Valley: One Man's Farm
description: A 60-second lesson on Stardew Valley: One Man's Farm.

hook.text: One person made Stardew Valley. Eric Barone coded, drew, composed, and wrote everything himself over 4 years.

buildup.text: Barone loved Harvest Moon but felt the series had declined. He decided to make his own farming game.

discovery.text: Working alone, he taught himself programming, pixel art, and music composition from online tutorials.

twist.text: Stardew Valley sold over 30 million copies. One person's passion project outsold most studio-made games.

climax.text: Barone continues updating it for free. He turned down deals that would compromise his creative vision.

punchline.text: Thirty million copies. One developer. Four years. Pure dedication.

quiz.question: How many copies has Stardew Valley sold?
